samples.text: Smoke can be produced by fog machines as well as fire. There is smoke coming from the stage. Does this guarantee there is a fire?
Not all smoke is caused by fire. Fog machines can also create smoke. Smoke is coming from the stage. Does this prove there is a fire?
Smoke may have multiple causes, including theatrical fog machines. Smoke is visible near the stage. Is fire certain to be present?
Visible airborne vapor effects can originate from artificial smoke generators as well as combustion. Smoke-like vapor is coming from the stage. Does this necessarily indicate combustion?

If an observation can be explained by multiple causes, then one specific cause cannot be concluded with certainty. Smoke can result from both fog machines and fire. Smoke is observed on the stage. Does this guarantee a fire?
Smoke can be produced by fog machines as well as fire. Concert tickets are often expensive. There is smoke coming from the stage. Does this guarantee there is a fire?
